4308 if (!response.Empty()) {
4309 object_sp = StructuredData::ParseJSON(response.GetStringRef());
4310 }
4311 }
4312 }
4313 }
4314 return object_sp;
4315}
4316
4317StructuredData::ObjectSP ProcessGDBRemote::GetDynamicLoaderProcessState() {
4318 StructuredData::ObjectSP object_sp;
4319 StructuredData::ObjectSP args_dict(new StructuredData::Dictionary());
4320
4321 if (m_gdb_comm.GetDynamicLoaderProcessStateSupported()) {
4322 StringExtractorGDBRemote response;
4323 response.SetResponseValidatorToJSON();
4324 if (m_gdb_comm.SendPacketAndWaitForResponse("jGetDyldProcessState",
4325 response) ==
4326 GDBRemoteCommunication::PacketResult::Success) {
4327 StringExtractorGDBRemote::ResponseType response_type =
4328 response.GetResponseType();
4329 if (response_type == StringExtractorGDBRemote::eResponse) {
4330 if (!response.Empty()) {
4331 object_sp = StructuredData::ParseJSON(response.GetStringRef());
4332 }
4333 }
4334 }
4335 }
4336 return object_sp;
4337}
4338
4339StructuredData::ObjectSP ProcessGDBRemote::GetSharedCacheInfo() {
4340 std::lock_guard<std::mutex> guard(m_shared_cache_info_mutex);
4341 StructuredData::ObjectSP args_dict(new StructuredData::Dictionary());
4342
4343 if (m_shared_cache_info_sp || !m_gdb_comm.GetSharedCacheInfoSupported())
4344 return m_shared_cache_info_sp;
4345
4346 StreamString packet;
4347 packet << "jGetSharedCacheInfo:";
4348 args_dict->Dump(packet, false);
4349
4350 StringExtractorGDBRemote response;
4351 response.SetResponseValidatorToJSON();
4352 if (m_gdb_comm.SendPacketAndWaitForResponse(packet.GetString(), response) ==
4353 GDBRemoteCommunication::PacketResult::Success) {
4354 StringExtractorGDBRemote::ResponseType response_type =
4355 response.GetResponseType();
4356 if (response_type == StringExtractorGDBRemote::eResponse) {
4357 if (response.Empty())
4358 return {};
4359 StructuredData::ObjectSP response_sp =
4360 StructuredData::ParseJSON(response.GetStringRef());
4361 if (!response_sp)
4362 return {};
4363 StructuredData::Dictionary *dict = response_sp->GetAsDictionary();
4364 if (!dict)
4365 return {};
4366 if (!dict->HasKey("shared_cache_uuid"))
4367 return {};
4368 llvm::StringRef uuid_str;
4369 if (!dict->GetValueForKeyAsString("shared_cache_uuid", uuid_str, "") ||
4370 uuid_str == "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000")
4371 return {};
4372 if (dict->HasKey("shared_cache_path")) {
4373 UUID uuid;
4374 uuid.SetFromStringRef(uuid_str);
4375 FileSpec sc_path(
4376 dict->GetValueForKey("shared_cache_path")->GetStringValue());
4377
4378 SymbolSharedCacheUse sc_mode =
4379 ModuleList::GetGlobalModuleListProperties()
4380 .GetSharedCacheBinaryLoading();
4381
4382 if (sc_mode == eSymbolSharedCacheUseHostAndInferiorSharedCache ||
4383 sc_mode == eSymbolSharedCacheUseInferiorSharedCacheOnly) {
4384 // Attempt to open the shared cache at sc_path, and
4385 // if the uuid matches, index all the files.
4386 HostInfo::SharedCacheIndexFiles(sc_path, uuid, sc_mode);
4387 }
4388 }
4389 m_shared_cache_info_sp = response_sp;
4390 }
4391 }
4392 return m_shared_cache_info_sp;
4393}
4394
4395Status ProcessGDBRemote::ConfigureStructuredData(
4396 llvm::StringRef type_name, const StructuredData::ObjectSP &config_sp) {
4397 return m_gdb_comm.ConfigureRemoteStructuredData(type_name, config_sp);
4398}
4399
4400// Establish the largest memory read/write payloads we should use. If the
4401// remote stub has a max packet size, stay under that size.
4402//
4403// If the remote stub's max packet size is crazy large, use a reasonable
4404// largeish default.
4405//
4406// If the remote stub doesn't advertise a max packet size, use a conservative
4407// default.
4408
4409void ProcessGDBRemote::GetMaxMemorySize() {
4410 const uint64_t reasonable_largeish_default = 128 * 1024;
4411 const uint64_t conservative_default = 512;
4412
4413 if (m_max_memory_size == 0) {
4414 uint64_t stub_max_size = m_gdb_comm.GetRemoteMaxPacketSize();
4415 if (stub_max_size != UINT64_MAX && stub_max_size != 0) {
4416 // Save the stub's claimed maximum packet size
4417 m_remote_stub_max_memory_size = stub_max_size;
4418
4419 // Even if the stub says it can support ginormous packets, don't exceed
4420 // our reasonable largeish default packet size.
4421 if (stub_max_size > reasonable_largeish_default) {
4422 stub_max_size = reasonable_largeish_default;
4423 }
4424
4425 // Memory packet have other overheads too like Maddr,size:#NN Instead of
4426 // calculating the bytes taken by size and addr every time, we take a
4427 // maximum guess here.
4428 if (stub_max_size > 70)
4429 stub_max_size -= 32 + 32 + 6;
4430 else {
4431 // In unlikely scenario that max packet size is less then 70, we will
4432 // hope that data being written is small enough to fit.
4433 Log *log(GetLog(GDBRLog::Comm | GDBRLog::Memory));
4434 if (log)
4435 log->Warning("Packet size is too small. "
4436 "LLDB may face problems while writing memory");
4437 }
4438
4439 m_max_memory_size = stub_max_size;
4440 } else {
4441 m_max_memory_size = conservative_default;
4442 }
4443 }
4444}
4445
4446void ProcessGDBRemote::SetUserSpecifiedMaxMemoryTransferSize(
4447 uint64_t user_specified_max) {
4448 if (user_specified_max != 0) {
4449 GetMaxMemorySize();
4450
4451 if (m_remote_stub_max_memory_size != 0) {
4452 if (m_remote_stub_max_memory_size < user_specified_max) {
4453 m_max_memory_size = m_remote_stub_max_memory_size; // user specified a
4454 // packet size too
4455 // big, go as big
4456 // as the remote stub says we can go.
4457 } else {
4458 m_max_memory_size = user_specified_max; // user's packet size is good
4459 }
4460 } else {
4461 m_max_memory_size =
4462 user_specified_max; // user's packet size is probably fine
4463 }
4464 }
4465}
4466
4467bool ProcessGDBRemote::GetModuleSpec(const FileSpec &module_file_spec,
4468 const ArchSpec &arch,
4469 ModuleSpec &module_spec) {
4470 Log *log = GetLog(LLDBLog::Platform);
4471
4472 const ModuleCacheKey key(module_file_spec.GetPath(),
4473 arch.GetTriple().getTriple());
4474 auto cached = m_cached_module_specs.find(key);
4475 if (cached != m_cached_module_specs.end()) {
4476 module_spec = cached->second;
4477 return bool(module_spec);
4478 }
4479
4480 if (!m_gdb_comm.GetModuleInfo(module_file_spec, arch, module_spec)) {
4481 LLDB_LOGF(log, "ProcessGDBRemote::%s - failed to get module info for %s:%s",
4482 __FUNCTION__, module_file_spec.GetPath().c_str(),
4483 arch.GetTriple().getTriple().c_str());
4484 return false;
4485 }
4486
4487 if (log) {
4488 StreamString stream;
4489 module_spec.Dump(stream);
4490 LLDB_LOGF(log, "ProcessGDBRemote::%s - got module info for (%s:%s) : %s",
4491 __FUNCTION__, module_file_spec.GetPath().c_str(),
4492 arch.GetTriple().getTriple().c_str(), stream.GetData());
4493 }
4494
4495 m_cached_module_specs[key] = module_spec;
4496 return true;
4497}
4498
4499void ProcessGDBRemote::PrefetchModuleSpecs(
4500 llvm::ArrayRef<FileSpec> module_file_specs, const llvm::Triple &triple) {
4501 auto module_specs = m_gdb_comm.GetModulesInfo(module_file_specs, triple);
4502 if (module_specs) {
4503 for (const FileSpec &spec : module_file_specs)
4504 m_cached_module_specs[ModuleCacheKey(spec.GetPath(),
4505 triple.getTriple())] = ModuleSpec();
4506 for (const ModuleSpec &spec : *module_specs)
4507 m_cached_module_specs[ModuleCacheKey(spec.GetFileSpec().GetPath(),
4508 triple.getTriple())] = spec;
4509 }
4510}
4511
4512llvm::VersionTuple ProcessGDBRemote::GetHostOSVersion() {
4513 return m_gdb_comm.GetOSVersion();
4514}
4515
4516llvm::VersionTuple ProcessGDBRemote::GetHostMacCatalystVersion() {
4517 return m_gdb_comm.GetMacCatalystVersion();
4518}
4519
4520namespace {
4521
4522typedef std::vector<std::string> stringVec;
4523
4524typedef std::vector<struct GdbServerRegisterInfo> GDBServerRegisterVec;
4525struct RegisterSetInfo {
4526 ConstString name;
4527};
4528
4529typedef std::map<uint32_t, RegisterSetInfo> RegisterSetMap;
4530
4531struct GdbServerTargetInfo {
4532 std::string arch;
4533 std::string osabi;
4534 stringVec includes;
4535 RegisterSetMap reg_set_map;
4536};
4537
4538static FieldEnum::Enumerators ParseEnumEvalues(const XMLNode &enum_node) {
4539 Log *log(GetLog(GDBRLog::Process));
4540 // We will use the last instance of each value. Also we preserve the order
4541 // of declaration in the XML, as it may not be numerical.
4542 // For example, hardware may initially release with two states that software
4543 // can read from a register field:
4544 // 0 = startup, 1 = running
4545 // If in a future hardware release, the designers added a pre-startup state:
4546 // 0 = startup, 1 = running, 2 = pre-startup
4547 // Now it makes more sense to list them in this logical order as opposed to
4548 // numerical order:
4549 // 2 = pre-startup, 1 = startup, 0 = startup
4550 // This only matters for "register info" but let's trust what the server
4551 // chose regardless.
4552 std::map<uint64_t, FieldEnum::Enumerator> enumerators;
4553
4554 enum_node.ForEachChildElementWithName(
4555 "evalue", [&enumerators, &log](const XMLNode &enumerator_node) {
4556 std::optional<llvm::StringRef> name;
4557 std::optional<uint64_t> value;
4558
4559 enumerator_node.ForEachAttribute(
4560 [&name, &value, &log](const llvm::StringRef &attr_name,
4561 const llvm::StringRef &attr_value) {
4562 if (attr_name == "name") {
4563 if (attr_value.size())
4564 name = attr_value;
4565 else
4566 LLDB_LOG(log, "ProcessGDBRemote::ParseEnumEvalues "
4567 "Ignoring empty name in evalue");
4568 } else if (attr_name == "value") {
4569 uint64_t parsed_value = 0;
4570 if (llvm::to_integer(attr_value, parsed_value))
4571 value = parsed_value;
4572 else
4573 LLDB_LOG(log,
4574 "ProcessGDBRemote::ParseEnumEvalues "
4575 "Invalid value \"{0}\" in "
4576 "evalue",
4577 attr_value.data());
4578 } else
4579 LLDB_LOG(log,
4580 "ProcessGDBRemote::ParseEnumEvalues Ignoring "
4581 "unknown attribute "
4582 "\"{0}\" in evalue",
4583 attr_name.data());
4584
4585 // Keep walking attributes.
4586 return true;
4587 });
4588
4589 if (value && name)
4590 enumerators.insert_or_assign(
4591 *value, FieldEnum::Enumerator(*value, name->str()));
4592
4593 // Find all evalue elements.
4594 return true;
4595 });
4596
4597 FieldEnum::Enumerators final_enumerators;
4598 for (auto [_, enumerator] : enumerators)
4599 final_enumerators.push_back(enumerator);
4600
4601 return final_enumerators;
4602}
4603
4604static void
4605ParseEnums(XMLNode feature_node,
4606 llvm::StringMap<std::unique_ptr<FieldEnum>> &registers_enum_types) {
4607 Log *log(GetLog(GDBRLog::Process));
4608
4609 // The top level element is "<enum...".
4610 feature_node.ForEachChildElementWithName(
4611 "enum", [log, &registers_enum_types](const XMLNode &enum_node) {
4612 std::string id;
4613
4614 enum_node.ForEachAttribute([&id](const llvm::StringRef &attr_name,
4615 const llvm::StringRef &attr_value) {
4616 if (attr_name == "id")
4617 id = attr_value;
4618
4619 // There is also a "size" attribute that is supposed to be the size in
4620 // bytes of the register this applies to. However:
4621 // * LLDB doesn't need this information.
4622 // * It is difficult to verify because you have to wait until the
4623 // enum is applied to a field.
4624 //
4625 // So we will emit this attribute in XML for GDB's sake, but will not
4626 // bother ingesting it.
4627
4628 // Walk all attributes.
4629 return true;
4630 });
4631
4632 if (!id.empty()) {
4633 FieldEnum::Enumerators enumerators = ParseEnumEvalues(enum_node);
4634 if (!enumerators.empty()) {
4635 LLDB_LOG(log,
4636 "ProcessGDBRemote::ParseEnums Found enum type \"{0}\"",
4637 id);
4638 registers_enum_types.insert_or_assign(
4639 id, std::make_unique<FieldEnum>(id, enumerators));
4640 }
4641 }
4642
4643 // Find all <enum> elements.
4644 return true;
4645 });
4646}
4647
4648static std::vector<RegisterFlags::Field> ParseFlagsFields(
4649 XMLNode flags_node, unsigned size,
4650 const llvm::StringMap<std::unique_ptr<FieldEnum>> &registers_enum_types) {
4651 Log *log(GetLog(GDBRLog::Process));
4652 const unsigned max_start_bit = size * 8 - 1;
4653
4654 // Process the fields of this set of flags.
4655 std::vector<RegisterFlags::Field> fields;
4656 flags_node.ForEachChildElementWithName("field", [&fields, max_start_bit, &log,
4657 &registers_enum_types](
4658 const XMLNode
4659 &field_node) {
4660 std::optional<llvm::StringRef> name;
4661 std::optional<unsigned> start;
4662 std::optional<unsigned> end;
4663 std::optional<llvm::StringRef> type;
4664
4665 field_node.ForEachAttribute([&name, &start, &end, &type, max_start_bit,
4666 &log](const llvm::StringRef &attr_name,
4667 const llvm::StringRef &attr_value) {
4668 // Note that XML in general requires that each of these attributes only
4669 // appears once, so we don't have to handle that here.
4670 if (attr_name == "name") {
4671 LLDB_LOG(
4672 log,
4673 "ProcessGDBRemote::ParseFlagsFields Found field node name \"{0}\"",
4674 attr_value.data());
4675 name = attr_value;
4676 } else if (attr_name == "start") {
4677 unsigned parsed_start = 0;
4678 if (llvm::to_integer(attr_value, parsed_start)) {
4679 if (parsed_start > max_start_bit) {
4680 LLDB_LOG(log,
4681 "ProcessGDBRemote::ParseFlagsFields Invalid start {0} in "
4682 "field node, "
4683 "cannot be > {1}",
4684 parsed_start, max_start_bit);
4685 } else
4686 start = parsed_start;
4687 } else {
4688 LLDB_LOG(
4689 log,
4690 "ProcessGDBRemote::ParseFlagsFields Invalid start \"{0}\" in "
4691 "field node",
4692 attr_value.data());
4693 }
4694 } else if (attr_name == "end") {
4695 unsigned parsed_end = 0;
4696 if (llvm::to_integer(attr_value, parsed_end))
4697 if (parsed_end > max_start_bit) {
4698 LLDB_LOG(log,
4699 "ProcessGDBRemote::ParseFlagsFields Invalid end {0} in "
4700 "field node, "
4701 "cannot be > {1}",
4702 parsed_end, max_start_bit);
4703 } else
4704 end = parsed_end;
4705 else {
4706 LLDB_LOG(log,
4707 "ProcessGDBRemote::ParseFlagsFields Invalid end \"{0}\" in "
4708 "field node",
4709 attr_value.data());
4710 }
4711 } else if (attr_name == "type") {
4712 type = attr_value;
4713 } else {
4714 LLDB_LOG(
4715 log,
4716 "ProcessGDBRemote::ParseFlagsFields Ignoring unknown attribute "
4717 "\"{0}\" in field node",
4718 attr_name.data());
4719 }
4720
4721 return true; // Walk all attributes of the field.
4722 });
4723
4724 if (name && start && end) {
4725 if (*start > *end)
4726 LLDB_LOG(
4727 log,
4728 "ProcessGDBRemote::ParseFlagsFields Start {0} > end {1} in field "
4729 "\"{2}\", ignoring",
4730 *start, *end, name->data());
4731 else {
4732 if (RegisterFlags::Field::GetSizeInBits(*start, *end) > 64)
4733 LLDB_LOG(log,
4734 "ProcessGDBRemote::ParseFlagsFields Ignoring field \"{2}\" "
4735 "that has "
4736 "size > 64 bits, this is not supported",
4737 name->data());
4738 else {
4739 // A field's type may be set to the name of an enum type.
4740 const FieldEnum *enum_type = nullptr;
4741 if (type && !type->empty()) {
4742 auto found = registers_enum_types.find(*type);
4743 if (found != registers_enum_types.end()) {
4744 enum_type = found->second.get();
4745
4746 // No enumerator can exceed the range of the field itself.
4747 uint64_t max_value =
4748 RegisterFlags::Field::GetMaxValue(*start, *end);
4749 for (const auto &enumerator : enum_type->GetEnumerators()) {
4750 if (enumerator.m_value > max_value) {
4751 enum_type = nullptr;
4752 LLDB_LOG(
4753 log,
4754 "ProcessGDBRemote::ParseFlagsFields In enum \"{0}\" "
4755 "evalue \"{1}\" with value {2} exceeds the maximum value "
4756 "of field \"{3}\" ({4}), ignoring enum",
4757 type->data(), enumerator.m_name, enumerator.m_value,
4758 name->data(), max_value);
4759 break;
4760 }
4761 }
4762 } else {
4763 LLDB_LOG(log,
4764 "ProcessGDBRemote::ParseFlagsFields Could not find type "
4765 "\"{0}\" "
4766 "for field \"{1}\", ignoring",
4767 type->data(), name->data());
4768 }
4769 }
4770
4771 fields.push_back(
4772 RegisterFlags::Field(name->str(), *start, *end, enum_type));
4773 }
4774 }
4775 }
4776
4777 return true; // Iterate all "field" nodes.
4778 });
4779 return fields;
4780}
4781
4782void ParseFlags(
4783 XMLNode feature_node,
4784 llvm::StringMap<std::unique_ptr<RegisterFlags>> &registers_flags_types,
4785 const llvm::StringMap<std::unique_ptr<FieldEnum>> &registers_enum_types) {
4786 Log *log(GetLog(GDBRLog::Process));
4787
4788 feature_node.ForEachChildElementWithName(
4789 "flags",
4790 [&log, &registers_flags_types,
4791 &registers_enum_types](const XMLNode &flags_node) -> bool {
4792 LLDB_LOG(log, "ProcessGDBRemote::ParseFlags Found flags node \"{0}\"",
4793 flags_node.GetAttributeValue("id").c_str());
4794
4795 std::optional<llvm::StringRef> id;
4796 std::optional<unsigned> size;
4797 flags_node.ForEachAttribute(
4798 [&id, &size, &log](const llvm::StringRef &name,
4799 const llvm::StringRef &value) {
4800 if (name == "id") {
4801 id = value;
4802 } else if (name == "size") {
4803 unsigned parsed_size = 0;
4804 if (llvm::to_integer(value, parsed_size))
4805 size = parsed_size;
4806 else {
4807 LLDB_LOG(log,
4808 "ProcessGDBRemote::ParseFlags Invalid size \"{0}\" "
4809 "in flags node",
4810 value.data());
4811 }
4812 } else {
4813 LLDB_LOG(log,
4814 "ProcessGDBRemote::ParseFlags Ignoring unknown "
4815 "attribute \"{0}\" in flags node",
4816 name.data());
4817 }
4818 return true; // Walk all attributes.
4819 });
4820
4821 if (id && size) {
4822 // Process the fields of this set of flags.
4823 std::vector<RegisterFlags::Field> fields =
4824 ParseFlagsFields(flags_node, *size, registers_enum_types);
4825 if (fields.size()) {
4826 // Sort so that the fields with the MSBs are first.
4827 std::sort(fields.rbegin(), fields.rend());
4828 std::vector<RegisterFlags::Field>::const_iterator overlap =
4829 std::adjacent_find(fields.begin(), fields.end(),
4830 [](const RegisterFlags::Field &lhs,
4831 const RegisterFlags::Field &rhs) {
4832 return lhs.Overlaps(rhs);
4833 });
4834
4835 // If no fields overlap, use them.
4836 if (overlap == fields.end()) {
4837 if (registers_flags_types.contains(*id)) {
4838 // In theory you could define some flag set, use it with a
4839 // register then redefine it. We do not know if anyone does
4840 // that, or what they would expect to happen in that case.
4841 //
4842 // LLDB chooses to take the first definition and ignore the rest
4843 // as waiting until everything has been processed is more
4844 // expensive and difficult. This means that pointers to flag
4845 // sets in the register info remain valid if later the flag set
4846 // is redefined. If we allowed redefinitions, LLDB would crash
4847 // when you tried to print a register that used the original
4848 // definition.
4849 LLDB_LOG(
4850 log,
4851 "ProcessGDBRemote::ParseFlags Definition of flags "
4852 "\"{0}\" shadows "
4853 "previous definition, using original definition instead.",
4854 id->data());
4855 } else {
4856 registers_flags_types.insert_or_assign(
4857 *id, std::make_unique<RegisterFlags>(id->str(), *size,
4858 std::move(fields)));
4859 }
4860 } else {
4861 // If any fields overlap, ignore the whole set of flags.
4862 std::vector<RegisterFlags::Field>::const_iterator next =
4863 std::next(overlap);
4864 LLDB_LOG(
4865 log,
4866 "ProcessGDBRemote::ParseFlags Ignoring flags because fields "
4867 "{0} (start: {1} end: {2}) and {3} (start: {4} end: {5}) "
4868 "overlap.",
4869 overlap->GetName().c_str(), overlap->GetStart(),
4870 overlap->GetEnd(), next->GetName().c_str(), next->GetStart(),
4871 next->GetEnd());
4872 }
4873 } else {
4874 LLDB_LOG(
4875 log,
4876 "ProcessGDBRemote::ParseFlags Ignoring definition of flags "
4877 "\"{0}\" because it contains no fields.",
4878 id->data());
4879 }
4880 }
4881
4882 return true; // Keep iterating through all "flags" elements.
4883 });
4884}
4885
4886bool ParseRegisters(
4887 XMLNode feature_node, GdbServerTargetInfo &target_info,
4888 std::vector<DynamicRegisterInfo::Register> &registers,
4889 llvm::StringMap<std::unique_ptr<RegisterFlags>> &registers_flags_types,
4890 llvm::StringMap<std::unique_ptr<FieldEnum>> &registers_enum_types) {
4891 if (!feature_node)
4892 return false;
4893
4894 Log *log(GetLog(GDBRLog::Process));
4895
4896 // Enums first because they are referenced by fields in the flags.
4897 ParseEnums(feature_node, registers_enum_types);
4898 for (const auto &enum_type : registers_enum_types)
4899 enum_type.second->DumpToLog(log);
4900
4901 ParseFlags(feature_node, registers_flags_types, registers_enum_types);
4902 for (const auto &flags : registers_flags_types)
4903 flags.second->DumpToLog(log);
4904
4905 feature_node.ForEachChildElementWithName(
4906 "reg",
4907 [&target_info, &registers, &registers_flags_types,
4908 log](const XMLNode &reg_node) -> bool {
4909 std::string gdb_group;
4910 std::string gdb_type;
4911 DynamicRegisterInfo::Register reg_info;
4912 bool encoding_set = false;
4913 bool format_set = false;
4914
4915 // FIXME: we're silently ignoring invalid data here
4916 reg_node.ForEachAttribute([&target_info, &gdb_group, &gdb_type,
4917 &encoding_set, &format_set, &reg_info,
4918 log](const llvm::StringRef &name,
4919 const llvm::StringRef &value) -> bool {
4920 if (name == "name") {
4921 reg_info.name.SetString(value);
4922 } else if (name == "bitsize") {
4923 if (llvm::to_integer(value, reg_info.byte_size))
4924 reg_info.byte_size =
4925 llvm::divideCeil(reg_info.byte_size, CHAR_BIT);
4926 } else if (name == "type") {
4927 gdb_type = value.str();
4928 } else if (name == "group") {
4929 gdb_group = value.str();
4930 } else if (name == "regnum") {
4931 llvm::to_integer(value, reg_info.regnum_remote);
4932 } else if (name == "offset") {
4933 llvm::to_integer(value, reg_info.byte_offset);
4934 } else if (name == "altname") {
4935 reg_info.alt_name.SetString(value);
4936 } else if (name == "encoding") {
4937 encoding_set = true;
4938 reg_info.encoding = Args::StringToEncoding(value, eEncodingUint);
4939 } else if (name == "format") {
4940 format_set = true;
4941 if (!OptionArgParser::ToFormat(value.data(), reg_info.format,
4942 nullptr)
4943 .Success())
4944 reg_info.format =
4945 llvm::StringSwitch<lldb::Format>(value)
4946 .Case("vector-sint8", eFormatVectorOfSInt8)
4947 .Case("vector-uint8", eFormatVectorOfUInt8)
4948 .Case("vector-sint16", eFormatVectorOfSInt16)
4949 .Case("vector-uint16", eFormatVectorOfUInt16)
4950 .Case("vector-sint32", eFormatVectorOfSInt32)
4951 .Case("vector-uint32", eFormatVectorOfUInt32)
4952 .Case("vector-float32", eFormatVectorOfFloat32)
4953 .Case("vector-uint64", eFormatVectorOfUInt64)
4954 .Case("vector-uint128", eFormatVectorOfUInt128)
4955 .Default(eFormatInvalid);
4956 } else if (name == "group_id") {
4957 uint32_t set_id = UINT32_MAX;
4958 llvm::to_integer(value, set_id);
4959 RegisterSetMap::const_iterator pos =
4960 target_info.reg_set_map.find(set_id);
4961 if (pos != target_info.reg_set_map.end())
4962 reg_info.set_name = pos->second.name;
4963 } else if (name == "gcc_regnum" || name == "ehframe_regnum") {
4964 llvm::to_integer(value, reg_info.regnum_ehframe);
4965 } else if (name == "dwarf_regnum") {
4966 llvm::to_integer(value, reg_info.regnum_dwarf);
4967 } else if (name == "generic") {
4968 reg_info.regnum_generic = Args::StringToGenericRegister(value);
4969 } else if (name == "value_regnums") {
4970 SplitCommaSeparatedRegisterNumberString(value, reg_info.value_regs,
4971 0);
4972 } else if (name == "invalidate_regnums") {
4973 SplitCommaSeparatedRegisterNumberString(
4974 value, reg_info.invalidate_regs, 0);
4975 } else {
4976 LLDB_LOGF(log,
4977 "ProcessGDBRemote::ParseRegisters unhandled reg "
4978 "attribute %s = %s",
4979 name.data(), value.data());
4980 }
4981 return true; // Keep iterating through all attributes
4982 });
4983
4984 if (!gdb_type.empty()) {
4985 // gdb_type could reference some flags type defined in XML.
4986 llvm::StringMap<std::unique_ptr<RegisterFlags>>::iterator it =
4987 registers_flags_types.find(gdb_type);
4988 if (it != registers_flags_types.end()) {
4989 auto flags_type = it->second.get();
4990 if (reg_info.byte_size == flags_type->GetSize())
4991 reg_info.flags_type = flags_type;
4992 else
4993 LLDB_LOGF(log,
4994 "ProcessGDBRemote::ParseRegisters Size of register "
4995 "flags %s (%d bytes) for "
4996 "register %s does not match the register size (%d "
4997 "bytes). Ignoring this set of flags.",
4998 flags_type->GetID().c_str(), flags_type->GetSize(),
4999 reg_info.name.AsCString(), reg_info.byte_size);
5000 }
5001
5002 // There's a slim chance that the gdb_type name is both a flags type
5003 // and a simple type. Just in case, look for that too (setting both
5004 // does no harm).
5005 if (!gdb_type.empty() && !(encoding_set || format_set)) {
5006 if (llvm::StringRef(gdb_type).starts_with("int")) {
5007 reg_info.format = eFormatHex;
5008 reg_info.encoding = eEncodingUint;
5009 } else if (gdb_type == "data_ptr" || gdb_type == "code_ptr") {
5010 reg_info.format = eFormatAddressInfo;
5011 reg_info.encoding = eEncodingUint;
5012 } else if (gdb_type == "float" || gdb_type == "ieee_single" ||
5013 gdb_type == "ieee_double") {
5014 reg_info.format = eFormatFloat;
5015 reg_info.encoding = eEncodingIEEE754;
5016 } else if (gdb_type == "aarch64v" ||
5017 llvm::StringRef(gdb_type).starts_with("vec") ||
5018 gdb_type == "i387_ext" || gdb_type == "uint128" ||
5019 reg_info.byte_size > 16) {
5020 // lldb doesn't handle 128-bit uints correctly (for ymm*h), so
5021 // treat them as vector (similarly to xmm/ymm).
5022 // We can fall back to handling anything else <= 128 bit as an
5023 // unsigned integer, more than that, call it a vector of bytes.
5024 // This can happen if we don't recognise the type for AArc64 SVE
5025 // registers.
5026 reg_info.format = eFormatVectorOfUInt8;
5027 reg_info.encoding = eEncodingVector;
5028 } else {
5029 LLDB_LOGF(
5030 log,
5031 "ProcessGDBRemote::ParseRegisters Could not determine lldb"
5032 "format and encoding for gdb type %s",
5033 gdb_type.c_str());
5034 }
5035 }
5036 }
5037
5038 // Only update the register set name if we didn't get a "reg_set"
5039 // attribute. "set_name" will be empty if we didn't have a "reg_set"
5040 // attribute.
5041 if (!reg_info.set_name) {
5042 if (!gdb_group.empty()) {
5043 reg_info.set_name.SetCString(gdb_group.c_str());
5044 } else {
5045 // If no register group name provided anywhere,
5046 // we'll create a 'general' register set
5047 reg_info.set_name.SetCString("general");
5048 }
5049 }
5050
5051 if (reg_info.byte_size == 0) {
5052 LLDB_LOGF(log,
5053 "ProcessGDBRemote::%s Skipping zero bitsize register %s",
5054 __FUNCTION__, reg_info.name.AsCString());
5055 } else
5056 registers.push_back(reg_info);
5057
5058 return true; // Keep iterating through all "reg" elements
5059 });
5060 return true;
5061}
5062
5063} // namespace
5064
5065// This method fetches a register description feature xml file from
5066// the remote stub and adds registers/register groupsets/architecture
5067// information to the current process. It will call itself recursively
5068// for nested register definition files. It returns true if it was able
5069// to fetch and parse an xml file.
5070bool ProcessGDBRemote::GetGDBServerRegisterInfoXMLAndProcess(
5071 ArchSpec &arch_to_use, std::string xml_filename,
5072 std::vector<DynamicRegisterInfo::Register> &registers) {
5073 // request the target xml file
5074 llvm::Expected<std::string> raw = m_gdb_comm.ReadExtFeature("features", xml_filename);
5075 if (errorToBool(raw.takeError()))
5076 return false;
5077
5078 XMLDocument xml_document;
5079
5080 if (xml_document.ParseMemory(raw->c_str(), raw->size(),
5081 xml_filename.c_str())) {
5082 GdbServerTargetInfo target_info;
5083 std::vector<XMLNode> feature_nodes;
5084
5085 // The top level feature XML file will start with a <target> tag.
5086 XMLNode target_node = xml_document.GetRootElement("target");
5087 if (target_node) {
5088 target_node.ForEachChildElement([&target_info, &feature_nodes](
5089 const XMLNode &node) -> bool {
5090 llvm::StringRef name = node.GetName();
5091 if (name == "architecture") {
5092 node.GetElementText(target_info.arch);
5093 } else if (name == "osabi") {
5094 node.GetElementText(target_info.osabi);
5095 } else if (name == "xi:include" || name == "include") {
5096 std::string href = node.GetAttributeValue("href");
5097 if (!href.empty())
5098 target_info.includes.push_back(href);
5099 } else if (name == "feature") {
5100 feature_nodes.push_back(node);
5101 } else if (name == "groups") {
5102 node.ForEachChildElementWithName(
5103 "group", [&target_info](const XMLNode &node) -> bool {
5104 uint32_t set_id = UINT32_MAX;
5105 RegisterSetInfo set_info;
5106
5107 node.ForEachAttribute(
5108 [&set_id, &set_info](const llvm::StringRef &name,
5109 const llvm::StringRef &value) -> bool {
5110 // FIXME: we're silently ignoring invalid data here
5111 if (name == "id")
5112 llvm::to_integer(value, set_id);
5113 if (name == "name")
5114 set_info.name = ConstString(value);
5115 return true; // Keep iterating through all attributes
5116 });
5117
5118 if (set_id != UINT32_MAX)
5119 target_info.reg_set_map[set_id] = set_info;
5120 return true; // Keep iterating through all "group" elements
5121 });
5122 }
5123 return true; // Keep iterating through all children of the target_node
5124 });
5125 } else {
5126 // In an included XML feature file, we're already "inside" the <target>
5127 // tag of the initial XML file; this included file will likely only have
5128 // a <feature> tag. Need to check for any more included files in this
5129 // <feature> element.
5130 XMLNode feature_node = xml_document.GetRootElement("feature");
5131 if (feature_node) {
5132 feature_nodes.push_back(feature_node);
5133 feature_node.ForEachChildElement([&target_info](
5134 const XMLNode &node) -> bool {
5135 llvm::StringRef name = node.GetName();
5136 if (name == "xi:include" || name == "include") {
5137 std::string href = node.GetAttributeValue("href");
5138 if (!href.empty())
5139 target_info.includes.push_back(href);
5140 }
5141 return true;
5142 });
5143 }
5144 }
5145
5146 // gdbserver does not implement the LLDB packets used to determine host
5147 // or process architecture. If that is the case, attempt to use
5148 // the <architecture/> field from target.xml, e.g.:
5149 //
5150 // <architecture>i386:x86-64</architecture> (seen from VMWare ESXi)
5151 // <architecture>arm</architecture> (seen from Segger JLink on unspecified
5152 // arm board)
5153 if (!arch_to_use.IsValid() && !target_info.arch.empty()) {
5154 // We don't have any information about vendor or OS.
5155 arch_to_use.SetTriple(llvm::StringSwitch<std::string>(target_info.arch)
5156 .Case("i386:x86-64", "x86_64")
5157 .Case("riscv:rv64", "riscv64")
5158 .Case("riscv:rv32", "riscv32")
5159 .Default(target_info.arch) +
5160 "--");
5161
5162 if (arch_to_use.IsValid())
5163 GetTarget().MergeArchitecture(arch_to_use);
5164 }
5165
5166 if (arch_to_use.IsValid()) {
5167 for (auto &feature_node : feature_nodes) {
5168 ParseRegisters(feature_node, target_info, registers,
5169 m_registers_flags_types, m_registers_enum_types);
5170 }
5171
5172 for (const auto &include : target_info.includes) {
5173 GetGDBServerRegisterInfoXMLAndProcess(arch_to_use, include,
5174 registers);
5175 }
5176 }
5177 } else {
5178 return false;
5179 }
5180 return true;
5181}
5182
5183void ProcessGDBRemote::AddRemoteRegisters(
5184 std::vector<DynamicRegisterInfo::Register> &registers,
5185 const ArchSpec &arch_to_use) {
5186 std::map<uint32_t, uint32_t> remote_to_local_map;
5187 uint32_t remote_regnum = 0;
5188 for (auto it : llvm::enumerate(registers)) {
5189 DynamicRegisterInfo::Register &remote_reg_info = it.value();
5190
5191 // Assign successive remote regnums if missing.
5192 if (remote_reg_info.regnum_remote == LLDB_INVALID_REGNUM)
5193 remote_reg_info.regnum_remote = remote_regnum;
5194
5195 // Create a mapping from remote to local regnos.
5196 remote_to_local_map[remote_reg_info.regnum_remote] = it.index();
5197
5198 remote_regnum = remote_reg_info.regnum_remote + 1;
5199 }
5200
5201 for (DynamicRegisterInfo::Register &remote_reg_info : registers) {
5202 auto proc_to_lldb = [&remote_to_local_map](uint32_t process_regnum) {
5203 auto lldb_regit = remote_to_local_map.find(process_regnum);
5204 return lldb_regit != remote_to_local_map.end() ? lldb_regit->second
5205 : LLDB_INVALID_REGNUM;
5206 };
5207
5208 llvm::transform(remote_reg_info.value_regs,
5209 remote_reg_info.value_regs.begin(), proc_to_lldb);
5210 llvm::transform(remote_reg_info.invalidate_regs,
5211 remote_reg_info.invalidate_regs.begin(), proc_to_lldb);
5212 }
5213
5214 // Don't use Process::GetABI, this code gets called from DidAttach, and
5215 // in that context we haven't set the Target's architecture yet, so the
5216 // ABI is also potentially incorrect.
5217 if (ABISP abi_sp = ABI::FindPlugin(shared_from_this(), arch_to_use))
5218 abi_sp->AugmentRegisterInfo(registers);
5219
5220 m_register_info_sp->SetRegisterInfo(std::move(registers), arch_to_use);
5221}
5222
5223// query the target of gdb-remote for extended target information returns
5224// true on success (got register definitions), false on failure (did not).
5225llvm::Error ProcessGDBRemote::GetGDBServerRegisterInfo(ArchSpec &arch_to_use) {
5226 // If the remote does not offer XML, does not matter if we would have been
5227 // able to parse it.
5228 if (!m_gdb_comm.GetQXferFeaturesReadSupported())
5229 return llvm::createStringError(
5230 llvm::inconvertibleErrorCode(),
5231 "the debug server does not support \"qXfer:features:read\"");
5232
5233 if (!XMLDocument::XMLEnabled())
5234 return llvm::createStringError(
5235 llvm::inconvertibleErrorCode(),
5236 "the debug server supports \"qXfer:features:read\", but LLDB does not "
5237 "have XML parsing enabled (check LLLDB_ENABLE_LIBXML2)");
5238
5239 // These hold register type information for the whole of target.xml.
5240 // target.xml may include further documents that
5241 // GetGDBServerRegisterInfoXMLAndProcess will recurse to fetch and process.
5242 // That's why we clear the cache here, and not in
5243 // GetGDBServerRegisterInfoXMLAndProcess. To prevent it being cleared on every
5244 // include read.
5245 m_registers_flags_types.clear();
5246 m_registers_enum_types.clear();
5247 std::vector<DynamicRegisterInfo::Register> registers;
5248 if (GetGDBServerRegisterInfoXMLAndProcess(arch_to_use, "target.xml",
5249 registers) &&
5250 // Target XML is not required to include register information.
5251 !registers.empty())
5252 AddRemoteRegisters(registers, arch_to_use);
5253
5254 return m_register_info_sp->GetNumRegisters() > 0
5255 ? llvm::ErrorSuccess()
5256 : llvm::createStringError(
5257 llvm::inconvertibleErrorCode(),
5258 "the debug server did not describe any registers");
5259}
5260
5261llvm::Expected<LoadedModuleInfoList> ProcessGDBRemote::GetLoadedModuleList() {
5262 // Make sure LLDB has an XML parser it can use first
5263 if (!XMLDocument::XMLEnabled())
5264 return llvm::createStringError(llvm::inconvertibleErrorCode(),
5265 "XML parsing not available");
5266
5267 Log *log = GetLog(LLDBLog::Process);
5268 LLDB_LOGF(log, "ProcessGDBRemote::%s", __FUNCTION__);
5269
5270 LoadedModuleInfoList list;
5271 GDBRemoteCommunicationClient &comm = m_gdb_comm;
5272 bool can_use_svr4 = GetGlobalPluginProperties().GetUseSVR4();
5273
5274 // check that we have extended feature read support
5275 if (can_use_svr4 && comm.GetQXferLibrariesSVR4ReadSupported()) {
5276 // request the loaded library list
5277 llvm::Expected<std::string> raw = comm.ReadExtFeature("libraries-svr4", "");
5278 if (!raw)
5279 return raw.takeError();
5280
5281 // parse the xml file in memory
5282 LLDB_LOGF(log, "parsing: %s", raw->c_str());
5283 XMLDocument doc;
5284
5285 if (!doc.ParseMemory(raw->c_str(), raw->size(), "noname.xml"))
5286 return llvm::createStringError(llvm::inconvertibleErrorCode(),
5287 "Error reading noname.xml");
5288
5289 XMLNode root_element = doc.GetRootElement("library-list-svr4");
5290 if (!root_element)
5291 return llvm::createStringError(
5292 llvm::inconvertibleErrorCode(),
5293 "Error finding library-list-svr4 xml element");
5294
5295 // main link map structure
5296 std::string main_lm = root_element.GetAttributeValue("main-lm");
5297 // FIXME: we're silently ignoring invalid data here
5298 if (!main_lm.empty())
5299 llvm::to_integer(main_lm, list.m_link_map);
5300
5301 root_element.ForEachChildElementWithName(
5302 "library", [log, &list](const XMLNode &library) -> bool {
5303 LoadedModuleInfoList::LoadedModuleInfo module;
5304
5305 // FIXME: we're silently ignoring invalid data here
5306 library.ForEachAttribute(
5307 [&module](const llvm::StringRef &name,
5308 const llvm::StringRef &value) -> bool {
5309 uint64_t uint_value = LLDB_INVALID_ADDRESS;
5310 if (name == "name")
5311 module.set_name(value.str());
5312 else if (name == "lm") {
5313 // the address of the link_map struct.
5314 llvm::to_integer(value, uint_value);
5315 module.set_link_map(uint_value);
5316 } else if (name == "l_addr") {
5317 // the displacement as read from the field 'l_addr' of the
5318 // link_map struct.
5319 llvm::to_integer(value, uint_value);
5320 module.set_base(uint_value);
5321 // base address is always a displacement, not an absolute
5322 // value.
5323 module.set_base_is_offset(true);
5324 } else if (name == "l_ld") {
5325 // the memory address of the libraries PT_DYNAMIC section.
5326 llvm::to_integer(value, uint_value);
5327 module.set_dynamic(uint_value);
5328 }
5329
5330 return true; // Keep iterating over all properties of "library"
5331 });
5332
5333 if (log) {
5334 std::string name;
5335 lldb::addr_t lm = 0, base = 0, ld = 0;
5336 bool base_is_offset;
5337
5338 module.get_name(name);
5339 module.get_link_map(lm);
5340 module.get_base(base);
5341 module.get_base_is_offset(base_is_offset);
5342 module.get_dynamic(ld);
5343
5344 LLDB_LOGF(log,
5345 "found (link_map:0x%08" PRIx64 ", base:0x%08" PRIx64
5346 "[%s], ld:0x%08" PRIx64 ", name:'%s')",
5347 lm, base, (base_is_offset ? "offset" : "absolute"), ld,
5348 name.c_str());
5349 }
5350
5351 list.add(module);
5352 return true; // Keep iterating over all "library" elements in the root
5353 // node
5354 });
5355
5356 if (log)
5357 LLDB_LOGF(log, "found %" PRId32 " modules in total",
5358 (int)list.m_list.size());
5359 return list;
5360 } else if (comm.GetQXferLibrariesReadSupported()) {
5361 // request the loaded library list
5362 llvm::Expected<std::string> raw = comm.ReadExtFeature("libraries", "");
5363
5364 if (!raw)
5365 return raw.takeError();
5366
5367 LLDB_LOGF(log, "parsing: %s", raw->c_str());
5368 XMLDocument doc;
5369
5370 if (!doc.ParseMemory(raw->c_str(), raw->size(), "noname.xml"))
5371 return llvm::createStringError(llvm::inconvertibleErrorCode(),
5372 "Error reading noname.xml");
5373
5374 XMLNode root_element = doc.GetRootElement("library-list");
5375 if (!root_element)
5376 return llvm::createStringError(llvm::inconvertibleErrorCode(),
5377 "Error finding library-list xml element");
5378
5379 // FIXME: we're silently ignoring invalid data here
5380 root_element.ForEachChildElementWithName(
5381 "library", [log, &list](const XMLNode &library) -> bool {
5382 LoadedModuleInfoList::LoadedModuleInfo module;
5383
5384 std::string name = library.GetAttributeValue("name");
5385 module.set_name(name);
5386
5387 // The base address of a given library will be the address of its
5388 // first section. Most remotes send only one section for Windows
5389 // targets for example.
5390 const XMLNode &section =
5391 library.FindFirstChildElementWithName("section");
5392 std::string address = section.GetAttributeValue("address");
5393 uint64_t address_value = LLDB_INVALID_ADDRESS;
5394 llvm::to_integer(address, address_value);
5395 module.set_base(address_value);
5396 // These addresses are absolute values.
5397 module.set_base_is_offset(false);
5398
5399 if (log) {
5400 std::string name;
5401 lldb::addr_t base = 0;
5402 bool base_is_offset;
5403 module.get_name(name);
5404 module.get_base(base);
5405 module.get_base_is_offset(base_is_offset);
5406
5407 LLDB_LOGF(log, "found (base:0x%08" PRIx64 "[%s], name:'%s')", base,
5408 (base_is_offset ? "offset" : "absolute"), name.c_str());
5409 }
5410
5411 list.add(module);
5412 return true; // Keep iterating over all "library" elements in the root
5413 // node
5414 });
5415
5416 if (log)
5417 LLDB_LOGF(log, "found %" PRId32 " modules in total",
5418 (int)list.m_list.size());
5419 return list;
5420 } else {
5421 return llvm::createStringError(llvm::inconvertibleErrorCode(),
5422 "Remote libraries not supported");
5423 }
5424}
5425
5426lldb::ModuleSP ProcessGDBRemote::LoadModuleAtAddress(const FileSpec &file,
5427 lldb::addr_t link_map,
5428 lldb::addr_t base_addr,
5429 bool value_is_offset) {
5430 DynamicLoader *loader = GetDynamicLoader();
5431 if (!loader)
5432 return nullptr;
5433
5434 return loader->LoadModuleAtAddress(file, link_map, base_addr,
5435 value_is_offset);
5436}
5437
5438llvm::Error ProcessGDBRemote::LoadModules() {
5439 using lldb_private::process_gdb_remote::ProcessGDBRemote;
5440
5441 // request a list of loaded libraries from GDBServer
5442 llvm::Expected<LoadedModuleInfoList> module_list = GetLoadedModuleList();
5443 if (!module_list)
5444 return module_list.takeError();
5445
5446 // get a list of all the modules
5447 ModuleList new_modules;
5448
5449 for (LoadedModuleInfoList::LoadedModuleInfo &modInfo : module_list->m_list) {
5450 std::string mod_name;
5451 lldb::addr_t mod_base;
5452 lldb::addr_t link_map;
5453 bool mod_base_is_offset;
5454
5455 bool valid = true;
5456 valid &= modInfo.get_name(mod_name);
5457 valid &= modInfo.get_base(mod_base);
5458 valid &= modInfo.get_base_is_offset(mod_base_is_offset);
5459 if (!valid)
5460 continue;
5461
5462 if (!modInfo.get_link_map(link_map))
5463 link_map = LLDB_INVALID_ADDRESS;
5464
5465 FileSpec file(mod_name);
5466 FileSystem::Instance().Resolve(file);
5467 lldb::ModuleSP module_sp =
5468 LoadModuleAtAddress(file, link_map, mod_base, mod_base_is_offset);
5469
5470 if (module_sp.get())
5471 new_modules.Append(module_sp);
5472 }
5473
5474 if (new_modules.GetSize() > 0) {
5475 ModuleList removed_modules;
5476 Target &target = GetTarget();
5477 ModuleList &loaded_modules = m_process->GetTarget().GetImages();
5478
5479 for (size_t i = 0; i < loaded_modules.GetSize(); ++i) {
5480 const lldb::ModuleSP loaded_module = loaded_modules.GetModuleAtIndex(i);
5481
5482 bool found = false;
5483 for (size_t j = 0; j < new_modules.GetSize(); ++j) {
5484 if (new_modules.GetModuleAtIndex(j).get() == loaded_module.get())
5485 found = true;
5486 }
5487
5488 // The main executable will never be included in libraries-svr4, don't
5489 // remove it
5490 if (!found &&
5491 loaded_module.get() != target.GetExecutableModulePointer()) {
5492 removed_modules.Append(loaded_module);
5493 }
5494 }
5495
5496 loaded_modules.Remove(removed_modules);
5497 m_process->GetTarget().ModulesDidUnload(removed_modules, false);
5498
5499 new_modules.ForEach([&target](const lldb::ModuleSP module_sp) {
5500 lldb_private::ObjectFile *obj = module_sp->GetObjectFile();
5501 if (!obj)
5502 return IterationAction::Continue;
5503
5504 if (obj->GetType() != ObjectFile::Type::eTypeExecutable)
5505 return IterationAction::Continue;
5506
5507 lldb::ModuleSP module_copy_sp = module_sp;
5508 target.SetExecutableModule(module_copy_sp, eLoadDependentsNo);
5509 return IterationAction::Stop;
5510 });
5511
5512 loaded_modules.AppendIfNeeded(new_modules);
5513 m_process->GetTarget().ModulesDidLoad(new_modules);
5514 }
5515
5516 return llvm::ErrorSuccess();
5517}
5518
5519Status ProcessGDBRemote::GetFileLoadAddress(const FileSpec &file,
5520 bool &is_loaded,
5521 lldb::addr_t &load_addr) {
5522 is_loaded = false;
5523 load_addr = LLDB_INVALID_ADDRESS;
5524
5525 std::string file_path = file.GetPath(false);
5526 if (file_path.empty())
5527 return Status::FromErrorString("Empty file name specified");
5528
5529 StreamString packet;
5530 packet.PutCString("qFileLoadAddress:");
5531 packet.PutStringAsRawHex8(file_path);
5532
5533 StringExtractorGDBRemote response;
5534 if (m_gdb_comm.SendPacketAndWaitForResponse(packet.GetString(), response) !=
5535 GDBRemoteCommunication::PacketResult::Success)
5536 return Status::FromErrorString("Sending qFileLoadAddress packet failed");
5537
5538 if (response.IsErrorResponse()) {
5539 if (response.GetError() == 1) {
5540 // The file is not loaded into the inferior
5541 is_loaded = false;
5542 load_addr = LLDB_INVALID_ADDRESS;
5543 return Status();
5544 }
5545
5546 return Status::FromErrorString(
5547 "Fetching file load address from remote server returned an error");
5548 }
5549
5550 if (response.IsNormalResponse()) {
5551 is_loaded = true;
5552 load_addr = response.GetHexMaxU64(false, LLDB_INVALID_ADDRESS);
5553 return Status();
5554 }
5555
5556 return Status::FromErrorString(
5557 "Unknown error happened during sending the load address packet");
5558}
5559
5560void ProcessGDBRemote::ModulesDidLoad(ModuleList &module_list) {
5561 // We must call the lldb_private::Process::ModulesDidLoad () first before we
5562 // do anything
5563 Process::ModulesDidLoad(module_list);
5564
5565 // After loading shared libraries, we can ask our remote GDB server if it
5566 // needs any symbols.
5567 m_gdb_comm.ServeSymbolLookups(this);
5568}
5569
5570void ProcessGDBRemote::HandleAsyncStdout(llvm::StringRef out) {
5571 AppendSTDOUT(out.data(), out.size());
5572}
5573
5574static const char *end_delimiter = "--end--;";
5575static const int end_delimiter_len = 8;
5576
5577void ProcessGDBRemote::HandleAsyncMisc(llvm::StringRef data) {
5578 std::string input = data.str(); // '1' to move beyond 'A'
5579 if (m_partial_profile_data.length() > 0) {
5580 m_partial_profile_data.append(input);
5581 input = m_partial_profile_data;
5582 m_partial_profile_data.clear();
5583 }
5584
5585 size_t found, pos = 0, len = input.length();
5586 while ((found = input.find(end_delimiter, pos)) != std::string::npos) {
5587 StringExtractorGDBRemote profileDataExtractor(
5588 input.substr(pos, found).c_str());
5589 std::string profile_data =
5590 HarmonizeThreadIdsForProfileData(profileDataExtractor);
5591 BroadcastAsyncProfileData(profile_data);
5592
5593 pos = found + end_delimiter_len;
5594 }
5595
5596 if (pos < len) {
5597 // Last incomplete chunk.
5598 m_partial_profile_data = input.substr(pos);
5599 }
5600}
5601
5602std::string ProcessGDBRemote::HarmonizeThreadIdsForProfileData(
5603 StringExtractorGDBRemote &profileDataExtractor) {
5604 std::map<uint64_t, uint32_t> new_thread_id_to_used_usec_map;
5605 std::string output;
5606 llvm::raw_string_ostream output_stream(output);
5607 llvm::StringRef name, value;
5608
5609 // Going to assuming thread_used_usec comes first, else bail out.
5610 while (profileDataExtractor.GetNameColonValue(name, value)) {
5611 if (name.compare("thread_used_id") == 0) {
5612 StringExtractor threadIDHexExtractor(value);
5613 uint64_t thread_id = threadIDHexExtractor.GetHexMaxU64(false, 0);
5614
5615 bool has_used_usec = false;
5616 uint32_t curr_used_usec = 0;
5617 llvm::StringRef usec_name, usec_value;
5618 uint32_t input_file_pos = profileDataExtractor.GetFilePos();
5619 if (profileDataExtractor.GetNameColonValue(usec_name, usec_value)) {
5620 if (usec_name == "thread_used_usec") {
5621 has_used_usec = true;
5622 usec_value.getAsInteger(0, curr_used_usec);
5623 } else {
5624 // We didn't find what we want, it is probably an older version. Bail
5625 // out.
5626 profileDataExtractor.SetFilePos(input_file_pos);
5627 }
5628 }
5629
5630 if (has_used_usec) {
5631 uint32_t prev_used_usec = 0;
5632 std::map<uint64_t, uint32_t>::iterator iterator =
5633 m_thread_id_to_used_usec_map.find(thread_id);
5634 if (iterator != m_thread_id_to_used_usec_map.end())
5635 prev_used_usec = iterator->second;
5636
5637 uint32_t real_used_usec = curr_used_usec - prev_used_usec;
5638 // A good first time record is one that runs for at least 0.25 sec
5639 bool good_first_time =
5640 (prev_used_usec == 0) && (real_used_usec > 250000);
5641 bool good_subsequent_time =
5642 (prev_used_usec > 0) &&
5643 ((real_used_usec > 0) || (HasAssignedIndexIDToThread(thread_id)));
5644
5645 if (good_first_time || good_subsequent_time) {
5646 // We try to avoid doing too many index id reservation, resulting in
5647 // fast increase of index ids.
5648
5649 output_stream << name << ":";
5650 int32_t index_id = AssignIndexIDToThread(thread_id);
5651 output_stream << index_id << ";";
5652
5653 output_stream << usec_name << ":" << usec_value << ";";
5654 } else {
5655 // Skip past 'thread_used_name'.
5656 llvm::StringRef local_name, local_value;
5657 profileDataExtractor.GetNameColonValue(local_name, local_value);
5658 }
5659
5660 // Store current time as previous time so that they can be compared
5661 // later.
5662 new_thread_id_to_used_usec_map[thread_id] = curr_used_usec;
5663 } else {
5664 // Bail out and use old string.
5665 output_stream << name << ":" << value << ";";
5666 }
5667 } else {
5668 output_stream << name << ":" << value << ";";
5669 }
5670 }
5671 output_stream << end_delimiter;
5672 m_thread_id_to_used_usec_map = new_thread_id_to_used_usec_map;
5673
5674 return output;
5675}
5676
5677void ProcessGDBRemote::HandleStopReply() {
5678 if (GetStopID() != 0)
5679 return;
5680
5681 if (GetID() == LLDB_INVALID_PROCESS_ID) {
5682 lldb::pid_t pid = m_gdb_comm.GetCurrentProcessID();
5683 if (pid != LLDB_INVALID_PROCESS_ID)
5684 SetID(pid);
5685 }
5686 BuildDynamicRegisterInfo(true);
5687}
5688
5689llvm::Expected<bool> ProcessGDBRemote::SaveCore(llvm::StringRef outfile) {
5690 if (!m_gdb_comm.GetSaveCoreSupported())
5691 return false;
5692
5693 StreamString packet;
5694 packet.PutCString("qSaveCore;path-hint:");
5695 packet.PutStringAsRawHex8(outfile);
5696
5697 StringExtractorGDBRemote response;
5698 if (m_gdb_comm.SendPacketAndWaitForResponse(packet.GetString(), response) ==
5699 GDBRemoteCommunication::PacketResult::Success) {
5700 // TODO: grab error message from the packet? StringExtractor seems to
5701 // be missing a method for that
5702 if (response.IsErrorResponse())
5703 return llvm::createStringError(llvm::inconvertibleErrorCode(),
5704 "qSaveCore returned an error");
5705
5706 std::string path;
5707
5708 // process the response
5709 for (auto x : llvm::split(response.GetStringRef(), ';')) {
5710 if (x.consume_front("core-path:"))
5711 StringExtractor(x).GetHexByteString(path);
5712 }
5713
5714 // verify that we've gotten what we need
5715 if (path.empty())
5716 return llvm::createStringError(llvm::inconvertibleErrorCode(),
5717 "qSaveCore returned no core path");
5718
5719 // now transfer the core file
5720 FileSpec remote_core{llvm::StringRef(path)};
5721 Platform &platform = *GetTarget().GetPlatform();
5722 Status error = platform.GetFile(remote_core, FileSpec(outfile));
5723
5724 if (platform.IsRemote()) {
5725 // NB: we unlink the file on error too
5726 platform.Unlink(remote_core);
5727 if (error.Fail())
5728 return error.ToError();
5729 }
5730
5731 return true;
5732 }
5733
5734 return llvm::createStringError(llvm::inconvertibleErrorCode(),
5735 "Unable to send qSaveCore");
5736}
5737
5738static const char *const s_async_json_packet_prefix = "JSON-async:";
5739
5740static StructuredData::ObjectSP
5741ParseStructuredDataPacket(llvm::StringRef packet) {
5742 Log *log = GetLog(GDBRLog::Process);
5743
5744 if (!packet.consume_front(s_async_json_packet_prefix)) {
5745 if (log) {
5746 LLDB_LOGF(
5747 log,
5748 "GDBRemoteCommunicationClientBase::%s() received $J packet "
5749 "but was not a StructuredData packet: packet starts with "
5750 "%s",
5751 __FUNCTION__,
5752 packet.slice(0, strlen(s_async_json_packet_prefix)).str().c_str());
5753 }
5754 return StructuredData::ObjectSP();
5755 }
5756
5757 // This is an asynchronous JSON packet, destined for a StructuredDataPlugin.
5758 StructuredData::ObjectSP json_sp = StructuredData::ParseJSON(packet);
5759 if (log) {
5760 if (json_sp) {
5761 StreamString json_str;
5762 json_sp->Dump(json_str, true);
5763 json_str.Flush();
5764 LLDB_LOGF(log,
5765 "ProcessGDBRemote::%s() "
5766 "received Async StructuredData packet: %s",
5767 __FUNCTION__, json_str.GetData());
5768 } else {
5769 LLDB_LOGF(log,
5770 "ProcessGDBRemote::%s"
5771 "() received StructuredData packet:"
5772 " parse failure",
5773 __FUNCTION__);
5774 }
5775 }
5776 return json_sp;
5777}
5778
5779void ProcessGDBRemote::HandleAsyncStructuredDataPacket(llvm::StringRef data) {
5780 auto structured_data_sp = ParseStructuredDataPacket(data);
5781 if (structured_data_sp)
5782 RouteAsyncStructuredData(structured_data_sp);
5783}
5784
5785class CommandObjectProcessGDBRemoteSpeedTest : public CommandObjectParsed {
5786public:
5787 CommandObjectProcessGDBRemoteSpeedTest(CommandInterpreter &interpreter)
5788 : CommandObjectParsed(interpreter, "process plugin packet speed-test",
5789 "Tests packet speeds of various sizes to determine "
5790 "the performance characteristics of the GDB remote "
5791 "connection. ",
5792 nullptr),
5793 m_option_group(),
5794 m_num_packets(LLDB_OPT_SET_1, false, "count", 'c', 0, eArgTypeCount,
5795 "The number of packets to send of each varying size "
5796 "(default is 1000).",
5797 1000),
5798 m_max_send(LLDB_OPT_SET_1, false, "max-send", 's', 0, eArgTypeCount,
5799 "The maximum number of bytes to send in a packet. Sizes "
5800 "increase in powers of 2 while the size is less than or "
5801 "equal to this option value. (default 1024).",
5802 1024),
5803 m_max_recv(LLDB_OPT_SET_1, false, "max-receive", 'r', 0, eArgTypeCount,
5804 "The maximum number of bytes to receive in a packet. Sizes "
5805 "increase in powers of 2 while the size is less than or "
5806 "equal to this option value. (default 1024).",
5807 1024),
5808 m_json(LLDB_OPT_SET_1, false, "json", 'j',
5809 "Print the output as JSON data for easy parsing.", false, true) {
5810 m_option_group.Append(&m_num_packets, LLDB_OPT_SET_ALL, LLDB_OPT_SET_1);
5811 m_option_group.Append(&m_max_send, LLDB_OPT_SET_ALL, LLDB_OPT_SET_1);
5812 m_option_group.Append(&m_max_recv, LLDB_OPT_SET_ALL, LLDB_OPT_SET_1);
5813 m_option_group.Append(&m_json, LLDB_OPT_SET_ALL, LLDB_OPT_SET_1);
5814 m_option_group.Finalize();
5815 }
5816
5817 ~CommandObjectProcessGDBRemoteSpeedTest() override = default;
5818
5819 Options *GetOptions() override { return &m_option_group; }
5820
5821 void DoExecute(Args &command, CommandReturnObject &result) override {
5822 const size_t argc = command.GetArgumentCount();
5823 if (argc == 0) {
5824 ProcessGDBRemote *process =
5825 (ProcessGDBRemote *)m_interpreter.GetExecutionContext()
5826 .GetProcessPtr();
5827 if (process) {
5828 StreamSP output_stream_sp = result.GetImmediateOutputStream();
5829 if (!output_stream_sp)
5830 output_stream_sp = m_interpreter.GetDebugger().GetAsyncOutputStream();
5831 result.SetImmediateOutputStream(output_stream_sp);
5832
5833 const uint32_t num_packets =
5834 (uint32_t)m_num_packets.GetOptionValue().GetCurrentValue();
5835 const uint64_t max_send = m_max_send.GetOptionValue().GetCurrentValue();
5836 const uint64_t max_recv = m_max_recv.GetOptionValue().GetCurrentValue();
5837 const bool json = m_json.GetOptionValue().GetCurrentValue();
5838 const uint64_t k_recv_amount =
5839 4 * 1024 * 1024; // Receive amount in bytes
5840 process->GetGDBRemote().TestPacketSpeed(
5841 num_packets, max_send, max_recv, k_recv_amount, json,
5842 output_stream_sp ? *output_stream_sp : result.GetOutputStream());
5843 result.SetStatus(eReturnStatusSuccessFinishResult);
5844 return;
5845 }
5846 } else {
5847 result.AppendErrorWithFormat("'%s' takes no arguments",
5848 m_cmd_name.c_str());
5849 }
5850 result.SetStatus(eReturnStatusFailed);
5851 }
5852
5853protected:
5854 OptionGroupOptions m_option_group;
5855 OptionGroupUInt64 m_num_packets;
5856 OptionGroupUInt64 m_max_send;
5857 OptionGroupUInt64 m_max_recv;
5858 OptionGroupBoolean m_json;
5859};
5860
5861class CommandObjectProcessGDBRemotePacketHistory : public CommandObjectParsed {
5862private:
5863public:
5864 CommandObjectProcessGDBRemotePacketHistory(CommandInterpreter &interpreter)
5865 : CommandObjectParsed(interpreter, "process plugin packet history",
5866 "Dumps the packet history buffer. ", nullptr) {}
5867
5868 ~CommandObjectProcessGDBRemotePacketHistory() override = default;
5869
5870 void DoExecute(Args &command, CommandReturnObject &result) override {
5871 ProcessGDBRemote *process =
5872 (ProcessGDBRemote *)m_interpreter.GetExecutionContext().GetProcessPtr();
5873 if (process) {
5874 process->DumpPluginHistory(result.GetOutputStream());
5875 result.SetStatus(eReturnStatusSuccessFinishResult);
5876 return;
5877 }
5878 result.SetStatus(eReturnStatusFailed);
5879 }
5880};
5881
5882class CommandObjectProcessGDBRemotePacketXferSize : public CommandObjectParsed {
5883private:
5884public:
5885 CommandObjectProcessGDBRemotePacketXferSize(CommandInterpreter &interpreter)
5886 : CommandObjectParsed(
5887 interpreter, "process plugin packet xfer-size",
5888 "Maximum size that lldb will try to read/write one one chunk.",
5889 nullptr) {
5890 AddSimpleArgumentList(eArgTypeUnsignedInteger);
5891 }
5892
5893 ~CommandObjectProcessGDBRemotePacketXferSize() override = default;
5894
5895 void DoExecute(Args &command, CommandReturnObject &result) override {
5896 const size_t argc = command.GetArgumentCount();
5897 if (argc == 0) {
5898 result.AppendErrorWithFormat("'%s' takes an argument to specify the max "
5899 "amount to be transferred when "
5900 "reading/writing",
5901 m_cmd_name.c_str());
5902 return;
5903 }
5904
5905 ProcessGDBRemote *process =
5906 (ProcessGDBRemote *)m_interpreter.GetExecutionContext().GetProcessPtr();
5907 if (process) {
5908 const char *packet_size = command.GetArgumentAtIndex(0);
5909 errno = 0;
5910 uint64_t user_specified_max = strtoul(packet_size, nullptr, 10);
5911 if (errno == 0 && user_specified_max != 0) {
5912 process->SetUserSpecifiedMaxMemoryTransferSize(user_specified_max);
5913 result.SetStatus(eReturnStatusSuccessFinishResult);
5914 return;
5915 }
5916 }
5917 result.SetStatus(eReturnStatusFailed);
5918 }
5919};
5920
5921class CommandObjectProcessGDBRemotePacketSend : public CommandObjectParsed {
5922private:
5923public:
5924 CommandObjectProcessGDBRemotePacketSend(CommandInterpreter &interpreter)
5925 : CommandObjectParsed(interpreter, "process plugin packet send",
5926 "Send a custom packet through the GDB remote "
5927 "protocol and print the answer. "
5928 "The packet header and footer will automatically "
5929 "be added to the packet prior to sending and "
5930 "stripped from the result.",
5931 nullptr) {
5932 AddSimpleArgumentList(eArgTypeNone, eArgRepeatStar);
5933 }
5934
5935 ~CommandObjectProcessGDBRemotePacketSend() override = default;
5936
5937 void DoExecute(Args &command, CommandReturnObject &result) override {
5938 const size_t argc = command.GetArgumentCount();
5939 if (argc == 0) {
5940 result.AppendErrorWithFormat(
5941 "'%s' takes a one or more packet content arguments",
5942 m_cmd_name.c_str());
5943 return;
5944 }
5945
5946 ProcessGDBRemote *process =
5947 (ProcessGDBRemote *)m_interpreter.GetExecutionContext().GetProcessPtr();
5948 if (process) {
5949 for (size_t i = 0; i < argc; ++i) {
5950 const char *packet_cstr = command.GetArgumentAtIndex(0);
5951 StringExtractorGDBRemote response;
5952 process->GetGDBRemote().SendPacketAndWaitForResponse(
5953 packet_cstr, response, process->GetInterruptTimeout());
5954 result.SetStatus(eReturnStatusSuccessFinishResult);
5955 Stream &output_strm = result.GetOutputStream();
5956 output_strm.Printf(" packet: %s\n", packet_cstr);
5957 std::string response_str = std::string(response.GetStringRef());
5958
5959 if (strstr(packet_cstr, "qGetProfileData") != nullptr) {
5960 response_str = process->HarmonizeThreadIdsForProfileData(response);
5961 }
5962
5963 if (response_str.empty())
5964 output_strm.PutCString("response: \nerror: UNIMPLEMENTED\n");
5965 else
5966 output_strm.Printf("response: %s\n", response.GetStringRef().data());
5967 }
5968 }
5969 }
5970};
5971
5972class CommandObjectProcessGDBRemotePacketMonitor : public CommandObjectRaw {
5973private:
5974public:
5975 CommandObjectProcessGDBRemotePacketMonitor(CommandInterpreter &interpreter)
5976 : CommandObjectRaw(interpreter, "process plugin packet monitor",
5977 "Send a qRcmd packet through the GDB remote protocol "
5978 "and print the response. "
5979 "The argument passed to this command will be hex "
5980 "encoded into a valid 'qRcmd' packet, sent and the "
5981 "response will be printed.") {}
5982
5983 ~CommandObjectProcessGDBRemotePacketMonitor() override = default;
5984
5985 void DoExecute(llvm::StringRef command,
5986 CommandReturnObject &result) override {
5987 if (command.empty()) {
5988 result.AppendErrorWithFormat("'%s' takes a command string argument",
5989 m_cmd_name.c_str());
5990 return;
5991 }
5992
5993 ProcessGDBRemote *process =
5994 (ProcessGDBRemote *)m_interpreter.GetExecutionContext().GetProcessPtr();
5995 if (process) {
5996 StreamString packet;
5997 packet.PutCString("qRcmd,");
5998 packet.PutBytesAsRawHex8(command.data(), command.size());
5999
6000 StringExtractorGDBRemote response;
6001 Stream &output_strm = result.GetOutputStream();
6002 process->GetGDBRemote().SendPacketAndReceiveResponseWithOutputSupport(
6003 packet.GetString(), response, process->GetInterruptTimeout(),
6004 [&output_strm](llvm::StringRef output) { output_strm << output; });
6005 result.SetStatus(eReturnStatusSuccessFinishResult);
6006 output_strm.Printf(" packet: %s\n", packet.GetData());
6007 const std::string &response_str = std::string(response.GetStringRef());
6008
6009 if (response_str.empty())
6010 output_strm.PutCString("response: \nerror: UNIMPLEMENTED\n");
6011 else
6012 output_strm.Printf("response: %s\n", response.GetStringRef().data());
6013 }
6014 }
6015};
6016
6017class CommandObjectProcessGDBRemotePacket : public CommandObjectMultiword {
6018private:
6019public:
6020 CommandObjectProcessGDBRemotePacket(CommandInterpreter &interpreter)
6021 : CommandObjectMultiword(interpreter, "process plugin packet",
6022 "Commands that deal with GDB remote packets.",
6023 nullptr) {
6024 LoadSubCommand(
6025 "history",
6026 CommandObjectSP(
6027 new CommandObjectProcessGDBRemotePacketHistory(interpreter)));
6028 LoadSubCommand(
6029 "send", CommandObjectSP(
6030 new CommandObjectProcessGDBRemotePacketSend(interpreter)));
6031 LoadSubCommand(
6032 "monitor",
6033 CommandObjectSP(
6034 new CommandObjectProcessGDBRemotePacketMonitor(interpreter)));
6035 LoadSubCommand(
6036 "xfer-size",
6037 CommandObjectSP(
6038 new CommandObjectProcessGDBRemotePacketXferSize(interpreter)));
6039 LoadSubCommand("speed-test",
6040 CommandObjectSP(new CommandObjectProcessGDBRemoteSpeedTest(
6041 interpreter)));
6042 }
6043
6044 ~CommandObjectProcessGDBRemotePacket() override = default;
6045};
6046
6047class CommandObjectMultiwordProcessGDBRemote : public CommandObjectMultiword {
6048public:
6049 CommandObjectMultiwordProcessGDBRemote(CommandInterpreter &interpreter)
6050 : CommandObjectMultiword(
6051 interpreter, "process plugin",
6052 "Commands for operating on a ProcessGDBRemote process.",
6053 "process plugin <subcommand> [<subcommand-options>]") {
6054 LoadSubCommand(
6055 "packet",
6056 CommandObjectSP(new CommandObjectProcessGDBRemotePacket(interpreter)));
6057 }
6058
6059 ~CommandObjectMultiwordProcessGDBRemote() override = default;
6060};
6061
6062CommandObject *ProcessGDBRemote::GetPluginCommandObject() {
6063 if (!m_command_sp)
6064 m_command_sp = std::make_shared<CommandObjectMultiwordProcessGDBRemote>(
6065 GetTarget().GetDebugger().GetCommandInterpreter());
6066 return m_command_sp.get();
6067}
6068
6069void ProcessGDBRemote::DidForkSwitchSoftwareBreakpoints(bool enable) {
6070 GetBreakpointSiteList().ForEach([this, enable](BreakpointSite *bp_site) {
6071 if (bp_site->IsEnabled() &&
6072 (bp_site->GetType() == BreakpointSite::eSoftware ||
6073 bp_site->GetType() == BreakpointSite::eExternal)) {
6074 m_gdb_comm.SendGDBStoppointTypePacket(
6075 eBreakpointSoftware, enable, bp_site->GetLoadAddress(),
6076 GetSoftwareBreakpointTrapOpcode(bp_site), GetInterruptTimeout());
6077 }
6078 });
6079}
6080
6081void ProcessGDBRemote::DidForkSwitchHardwareTraps(bool enable) {
6082 if (m_gdb_comm.SupportsGDBStoppointPacket(eBreakpointHardware)) {
6083 GetBreakpointSiteList().ForEach([this, enable](BreakpointSite *bp_site) {
6084 if (bp_site->IsEnabled() &&
6085 bp_site->GetType() == BreakpointSite::eHardware) {
6086 m_gdb_comm.SendGDBStoppointTypePacket(
6087 eBreakpointHardware, enable, bp_site->GetLoadAddress(),
6088 GetSoftwareBreakpointTrapOpcode(bp_site), GetInterruptTimeout());
6089 }
6090 });
6091 }
6092
6093 for (const auto &wp_res_sp : m_watchpoint_resource_list.Sites()) {
6094 addr_t addr = wp_res_sp->GetLoadAddress();
6095 size_t size = wp_res_sp->GetByteSize();
6096 GDBStoppointType type = GetGDBStoppointType(wp_res_sp);
6097 m_gdb_comm.SendGDBStoppointTypePacket(type, enable, addr, size,
6098 GetInterruptTimeout());
6099 }
6100}
6101
6102void ProcessGDBRemote::DidFork(lldb::pid_t child_pid, lldb::tid_t child_tid) {
6103 Log *log = GetLog(GDBRLog::Process);
6104
6105 lldb::pid_t parent_pid = m_gdb_comm.GetCurrentProcessID();
6106 // Any valid TID will suffice, thread-relevant actions will set a proper TID
6107 // anyway.
6108 lldb::tid_t parent_tid = m_thread_ids.front();
6109
6110 lldb::pid_t follow_pid, detach_pid;
6111 lldb::tid_t follow_tid, detach_tid;
6112
6113 switch (GetFollowForkMode()) {
6114 case eFollowParent:
6115 follow_pid = parent_pid;
6116 follow_tid = parent_tid;
6117 detach_pid = child_pid;
6118 detach_tid = child_tid;
6119 break;
6120 case eFollowChild:
6121 follow_pid = child_pid;
6122 follow_tid = child_tid;
6123 detach_pid = parent_pid;
6124 detach_tid = parent_tid;
6125 break;
6126 }
6127
6128 // Switch to the process that is going to be detached.
6129 if (!m_gdb_comm.SetCurrentThread(detach_tid, detach_pid)) {
6130 LLDB_LOG(log, "ProcessGDBRemote::DidFork() unable to set pid/tid");
6131 return;
6132 }
6133
6134 // Disable all software breakpoints in the forked process.
6135 if (m_gdb_comm.SupportsGDBStoppointPacket(eBreakpointSoftware))
6136 DidForkSwitchSoftwareBreakpoints(false);
6137
6138 // Remove hardware breakpoints / watchpoints from parent process if we're
6139 // following child.
6140 if (GetFollowForkMode() == eFollowChild)
6141 DidForkSwitchHardwareTraps(false);
6142
6143 // Switch to the process that is going to be followed
6144 if (!m_gdb_comm.SetCurrentThread(follow_tid, follow_pid) ||
6145 !m_gdb_comm.SetCurrentThreadForRun(follow_tid, follow_pid)) {
6146 LLDB_LOG(log, "ProcessGDBRemote::DidFork() unable to reset pid/tid");
6147 return;
6148 }
6149
6150 LLDB_LOG(log, "Detaching process {0}", detach_pid);
6151 Status error = m_gdb_comm.Detach(false, detach_pid);
6152 if (error.Fail()) {
6153 LLDB_LOG(log, "ProcessGDBRemote::DidFork() detach packet send failed: {0}",
6154 error.AsCString() ? error.AsCString() : "<unknown error>");
6155 return;
6156 }
6157
6158 // Hardware breakpoints/watchpoints are not inherited implicitly,
6159 // so we need to readd them if we're following child.
6160 if (GetFollowForkMode() == eFollowChild) {
6161 DidForkSwitchHardwareTraps(true);
6162 // Update our PID
6163 SetID(child_pid);
6164 }
6165}
6166
6167void ProcessGDBRemote::DidVFork(lldb::pid_t child_pid, lldb::tid_t child_tid) {
6168 Log *log = GetLog(GDBRLog::Process);
6169
6170 LLDB_LOG(
6171 log,
6172 "ProcessGDBRemote::DidFork() called for child_pid: {0}, child_tid {1}",
6173 child_pid, child_tid);
6174 ++m_vfork_in_progress_count;
6175
6176 // Disable all software breakpoints for the duration of vfork.
6177 if (m_gdb_comm.SupportsGDBStoppointPacket(eBreakpointSoftware))
6178 DidForkSwitchSoftwareBreakpoints(false);
6179
6180 lldb::pid_t detach_pid;
6181 lldb::tid_t detach_tid;
6182
6183 switch (GetFollowForkMode()) {
6184 case eFollowParent:
6185 detach_pid = child_pid;
6186 detach_tid = child_tid;
6187 break;
6188 case eFollowChild:
6189 detach_pid = m_gdb_comm.GetCurrentProcessID();
6190 // Any valid TID will suffice, thread-relevant actions will set a proper TID
6191 // anyway.
6192 detach_tid = m_thread_ids.front();
6193
6194 // Switch to the parent process before detaching it.
6195 if (!m_gdb_comm.SetCurrentThread(detach_tid, detach_pid)) {
6196 LLDB_LOG(log, "ProcessGDBRemote::DidFork() unable to set pid/tid");
6197 return;
6198 }
6199
6200 // Remove hardware breakpoints / watchpoints from the parent process.
6201 DidForkSwitchHardwareTraps(false);
6202
6203 // Switch to the child process.
6204 if (!m_gdb_comm.SetCurrentThread(child_tid, child_pid) ||
6205 !m_gdb_comm.SetCurrentThreadForRun(child_tid, child_pid)) {
6206 LLDB_LOG(log, "ProcessGDBRemote::DidFork() unable to reset pid/tid");
6207 return;
6208 }
6209 break;
6210 }
6211
6212 LLDB_LOG(log, "Detaching process {0}", detach_pid);
6213 Status error = m_gdb_comm.Detach(false, detach_pid);
6214 if (error.Fail()) {
6215 LLDB_LOG(log,
6216 "ProcessGDBRemote::DidFork() detach packet send failed: {0}",
6217 error.AsCString() ? error.AsCString() : "<unknown error>");
6218 return;
6219 }
6220
6221 if (GetFollowForkMode() == eFollowChild) {
6222 // Update our PID
6223 SetID(child_pid);
6224 }
6225}
6226
6227void ProcessGDBRemote::DidVForkDone() {
6228 assert(m_vfork_in_progress_count > 0);
6229 --m_vfork_in_progress_count;
6230
6231 // Reenable all software breakpoints that were enabled before vfork.
6232 if (m_gdb_comm.SupportsGDBStoppointPacket(eBreakpointSoftware))
6233 DidForkSwitchSoftwareBreakpoints(true);
6234}
6235
6236void ProcessGDBRemote::DidExec() {
6237 // If we are following children, vfork is finished by exec (rather than
6238 // vforkdone that is submitted for parent).
6239 if (GetFollowForkMode() == eFollowChild) {
6240 if (m_vfork_in_progress_count > 0)
6241 --m_vfork_in_progress_count;
6242 }
6243 Process::DidExec();
6244}
